task_completion.description: Verify the conditions CSV file is created with the correct columns and trial ratios.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a spreadsheet or text editor showing 'oddball_conditions.csv' with columns for frequency, condition type, and trigger code. 80% of the rows should be Standard trials (440Hz, Trigger 10) and 20% should be Target trials (880Hz, Trigger 20).

task_completion.description: Verify the PsychoPy experiment file is created and named correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the PsychoPy Builder window. The window title or save dialog should indicate the file is saved as 'p300_oddball.psyexp' in the /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify a trial loop is set up referencing the conditions file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the PsychoPy Flow panel showing a loop around a routine. The loop properties window must show 'oddball_conditions.csv' loaded in the 'Conditions' field.

task_completion.description: Verify the sound component is configured with a 0.2s duration and variable frequency.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Sound component properties window in PsychoPy. The duration (Stop time) must be set to 0.2s, and the Sound/Frequency field must be set to a variable corresponding to the conditions file (e.g., $frequency).

task_completion.description: Verify a keyboard response component is included in the trial routine.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Keyboard component icon present in the trial routine timeline within the PsychoPy Builder interface.

task_completion.description: Verify the parallel port trigger is configured with the correct address, timing, and trigger variable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Parallel Port component properties window. The 'Port address' must be 0x0378, 'Start Data' must be set to the trigger variable (e.g., $trigger_code), and the 'Start time' must exactly match the start time of the sound component.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy Builder interface should be visible in the screenshots, showing the visual construction of routines and flow, rather than just a terminal window running a script to generate the .psyexp file.

integrity.description: Ensure the conditions file was genuinely created and edited.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor, spreadsheet application, or terminal editor (like nano/vim) actively editing the oddball_conditions.csv file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the Builder by manually writing the raw XML/Psyexp file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with PsychoPy component dialog boxes (Sound, Keyboard, Parallel Port) rather than a text editor modifying the raw .psyexp XML structure.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Experiment file must be saved exactly at: /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/p300_oddball.psyexp. Conditions file must be saved exactly at: /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/oddball_conditions.csv. The conditions file must contain 80% standard trials (trigger code 10) and 20% target trials (trigger code 20). The parallel port address in the trigger component must be exactly 0x0378.
